summary: Palestinian national poet. Known for 'Identity Card,' 'A Lover from Palestine,' Memory for Forgetfulness, Why Did You Leave the Horse Alone?, and the poetry collections The Butterfly's Burden and Mural. Closely associated with the Palestinian cause.

works.name: Poetry Collections
works.description: Major collections including The Butterfly's Burden, Mural, and earlier resistance poetry.

clues.clue: 'The Damascene Collar of the Dove' provides the title of The Butterfly's Burden collection
examples: 'Poems become diaphanous' in this city in a poem titled for its 'Collar of the Dove'

clues.clue: Palestinian national poet associated with resistance poetry

comprehensive_summary: Mahmoud Darwish (1941-2008) was the foremost Palestinian poet, closely associated with Palestinian national identity and resistance. His poetry collections include The Butterfly's Burden (containing 'The Damascene Collar of the Dove'), Mural, and Why Did You Leave the Horse Alone? He also wrote the prose work Memory for Forgetfulness about the 1982 siege of Beirut.

cards.front: Poet: Palestinian national poet; The Butterfly's Burden contains 'The Damascene Collar of the Dove'
